title: salix alba var. viridis
intro: Salix alba var. viridis, also known as the white willow tree or green willow, is a variety of the white willow tree (Salix alba) that is native to Europe and Western Asia. The bark of this tree has been used in traditional medicine for its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, containing salicin, a compound similar to aspirin. The medicinal properties of Salix alba var. viridis have been shown to provide health benefits such as reducing pain and inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and aiding in the treatment of fever and rheumatism. The herbal preparations of Salix alba var. viridis include teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to alleviate various health conditions.
